title: rafnia angulata subsp. ericifolia
intro: Rafnia angulata subsp. ericifolia, commonly known as the Rafnia or "Hottentot's Button Plant", is a low-growing, succulent plant native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Rafnia angulata subsp. ericifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
